title: melaleuca quadrifida subsp. quadrifida
intro: Melaleuca quadrifida subsp. quadrifida, also known as the prickly paperbark or prickly tea tree, is a shrub-like plant native to northern Australia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Melaleuca quadrifida subsp. quadrifida include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
